---
title: "Building My Personal AI Utilization System"
date: 2026-03-12
description: "A practical architecture for using AI tools in research, learning, and software development."
toc: true
---

<div class="language-toggle" role="group" aria-label="Language switch"><a class="language-toggle__link is-active" href="/articles/building-my-personal-ai-utilization-system/en.html" lang="en">EN</a><a class="language-toggle__link" href="/articles/building-my-personal-ai-utilization-system/ko.html" lang="ko">KO</a></div>

## Motivation
::: {.section-lead}
AI tools are powerful, but using them independently often leads to messy workflows.
:::

A typical workflow might look like this:

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Ask ChatGPT</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Open Perplexity</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Upload to NotebookLM</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Copy to Drive</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Repeat</span>
:::

::: {.symptom-block}
<p class="symptom-block__label">What breaks over time</p>

- context becomes fragmented
- knowledge spreads across multiple tools
- switching between AIs creates friction
- long-term work lacks a persistent structure
:::

The limitation is not the capability of AI models.

::: {.section-conclusion}
The real problem is the lack of a clear architecture for using them together.
:::

## Goal
::: {.section-lead}
Design a system where multiple AI tools cooperate instead of being used independently.
:::

The system should support:

- studying university courses
- managing research materials
- writing long documents
- building software projects
- organizing knowledge over time

::: {.section-conclusion}
The focus is practical workflow, not theoretical design.
:::

## Key Idea
::: {.section-lead}
Instead of relying on a single AI tool for everything, the system assigns specific roles to different tools.
:::

Example role distribution:

| Function | Tool |
|---|---|
| Search | Perplexity |
| Store | Zotero |
| Analyze | NotebookLM |
| Organize | Gemini |
| Design | Claude |
| Build | ChatGPT / Codex |
| Code State | GitHub |
| Memory | Google Drive |
| Control | gws CLI |

::: {.section-conclusion}
Each tool is used for what it does best, which turns a loose collection of AI products into a structured working environment.
:::

## Philosophy
::: {.section-lead}
The system follows three basic principles.
:::

<ol class="ordered-list">
  <li>
    <span class="ordered-list__title">AI assists thinking - it does not replace it</span>
    <span class="ordered-list__body">Human judgment remains central.</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="ordered-list__title">AI is a cognitive tool</span>
    <span class="ordered-list__body">AI extends research, coding, and learning workflows.</span>
  </li>
  <li>
    <span class="ordered-list__title">Working systems &gt; perfect systems</span>
    <span class="ordered-list__body">A simple system that works consistently is better than a perfect design that is never used.</span>
  </li>
</ol>

::: {.section-conclusion}
Keep judgment human, give each tool a clear job, and optimize for a system you will actually use.
:::

## Architecture Overview
::: {.section-lead}
Google Drive acts as the shared knowledge and memory layer of the system.
:::

The main operational interface to this layer is Gemini CLI, which interacts with Google Drive through gws.

The architecture follows a simple pattern:

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Inputs</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Gemini CLI</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Google Drive</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">AI tools</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Outputs</span>
:::

GitHub operates in parallel as the code-state system.

::: {.section-conclusion}
Raw materials enter the system, Gemini organizes them, AI tools consume the organized state, and the resulting outputs are written back into that same shared layer.
:::

## Architecture Diagram
```{mermaid fig-cap="System flow from raw inputs to shared memory, specialized AI tools, and final outputs."}
%%{init: {'theme': 'base', 'themeVariables': { 'darkMode': true, 'fontSize': '18px', 'fontFamily': 'IBM Plex Sans, sans-serif' }}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef default fill:#1E293B,stroke:#475569,stroke-width:1px,color:#F8FAFC,rx:8px,ry:8px,font-size:16px;
    classDef coreBox fill:#0F172A,stroke:#94A3B8,stroke-width:2px,color:#FFFFFF,font-weight:bold,font-size:18px;
    classDef endPoint fill:none,stroke:none,color:#94A3B8,font-size:18px,font-weight:bold;

    INPUTS["<b>INPUTS</b><br/><br/>LearnUS files &nbsp;|&nbsp; Research papers &nbsp;|&nbsp; Notes &nbsp;|&nbsp; Manuals<br/>Course PDFs &nbsp;|&nbsp; Project docs &nbsp;|&nbsp; Drafts &nbsp;|&nbsp; Outputs"]

    CLI["<b>Gemini CLI + gws integration</b><br/><br/>- file classification<br/>- renaming<br/>- folder routing<br/>- context updates<br/>- schedule extraction"]:::coreBox

    DRIVE["<b>Google Drive</b><br/>(Shared Memory Layer)<br/><br/>University/<br/>Projects/<br/>Librarian/<br/>librarian_memory/"]:::coreBox

    INPUTS --> CLI
    CLI --> DRIVE

    subgraph K_Tools ["Knowledge Tools"]
        direction TB
        N["NotebookLM"]
        P["Perplexity"]
        Z["Zotero - manual"]
    end

    subgraph E_Tools ["Execution Tools"]
        direction TB
        C["Claude"]
        GPT["ChatGPT / Codex"]
        CC["Claude Code"]
    end

    DRIVE --> K_Tools
    DRIVE --> E_Tools

    RA["Research & Analysis"]:::endPoint
    DI["Design & Implementation"]:::endPoint

    K_Tools --> RA
    E_Tools --> DI

    style K_Tools fill:#1E293B,stroke:#475569,stroke-width:1px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5,color:#CBD5E1,rx:8px,ry:8px
    style E_Tools fill:#1E293B,stroke:#475569,stroke-width:1px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5,color:#CBD5E1,rx:8px,ry:8px
```

## Core Architectural Principle
::: {.section-lead}
The system separates two types of state.
:::

### Knowledge State
Stored in Google Drive

Includes:

- course materials
- research summaries
- documentation
- project notes
- shared AI context

### Code State
Stored in GitHub

Includes:

- source code
- version history
- software projects

::: {.section-conclusion}
This separation keeps research workflows and development workflows clean.
:::

## Google Drive Folder Architecture
::: {.section-lead}
Google Drive acts as the shared memory layer of the system.
:::

All AI tools interact with this layer either directly or indirectly.

The structure is intentionally minimal.

### Root Structure
<div class="file-tree" aria-label="AI_OS root structure">
  <ul class="file-tree__root">
    <li class="file-tree__item">
      <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
        <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">AI_OS</span></button>
        <ul class="file-tree__children">
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">University</span></div></li>
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">Projects</span></div></li>
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">Librarian</span></div></li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>

Purpose of each folder:

- University &rarr; academic course materials
- Projects &rarr; personal and development projects
- Librarian &rarr; global AI system documentation

### Librarian
The Librarian folder contains system-level context for the AI utilization system.

Typical files:

<div class="file-tree" aria-label="Librarian folder structure">
  <ul class="file-tree__root">
    <li class="file-tree__item">
      <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
        <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">Librarian</span></button>
        <ul class="file-tree__children">
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--file"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--file" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">architecture.md</span></div></li>
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--file"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--file" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">routing.md</span></div></li>
          <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--file"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--file" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">decisions.md</span></div></li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>

File roles:

- architecture.md &rarr; describes the AI system architecture
- routing.md &rarr; rules for selecting the appropriate AI tool
- decisions.md &rarr; important system conventions and design choices

This folder acts as the global reference layer for the AI system.

### Local librarian_memory
Unlike Librarian, local librarian_memory folders exist inside each course or project.

Example:

<div class="file-tree" aria-label="Local librarian memory examples">
  <ul class="file-tree__root">
    <li class="file-tree__item">
      <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
        <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">University</span></button>
        <ul class="file-tree__children">
          <li class="file-tree__item">
            <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
              <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">2026_Spring</span></button>
              <ul class="file-tree__children">
                <li class="file-tree__item">
                  <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
                    <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">CAS2101_Discrete_Mathematics</span></button>
                    <ul class="file-tree__children">
                      <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">librarian_memory</span></div></li>
                    </ul>
                  </div>
                </li>
              </ul>
            </div>
          </li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </li>
    <li class="file-tree__item">
      <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
        <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">Projects</span></button>
        <ul class="file-tree__children">
          <li class="file-tree__item">
            <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
              <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">Personal_Website</span></button>
              <ul class="file-tree__children">
                <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">librarian_memory</span></div></li>
              </ul>
            </div>
          </li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>

These folders store local working context.

Typical contents:

- current task
- progress state
- important notes
- AI handoff information

Memory separation:

- Global context &rarr; Librarian/
- Local context &rarr; librarian_memory/

## Gemini-Managed Memory
::: {.section-lead}
Both `Librarian/` and local `librarian_memory/` folders should be managed automatically by Gemini.
:::

Through Gemini CLI + gws, Gemini can:

- organize files
- maintain task context
- summarize documents
- update working memory
- preserve continuity across sessions

::: {.section-conclusion}
In this architecture, Gemini acts as the system librarian.
:::

## University Workspace
::: {.section-lead}
Course materials are stored under the `University` directory.
:::

Example structure:

<div class="file-tree" aria-label="University workspace structure">
  <ul class="file-tree__root">
    <li class="file-tree__item">
      <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
        <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">University</span></button>
        <ul class="file-tree__children">
          <li class="file-tree__item">
            <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
              <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">2026_Spring</span></button>
              <ul class="file-tree__children">
                <li class="file-tree__item">
                  <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
                    <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">CAS2101_Discrete_Mathematics</span></button>
                    <ul class="file-tree__children">
                      <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">lectures</span></div></li>
                      <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">assignments</span></div></li>
                      <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">announcements</span></div></li>
                      <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">outputs</span></div></li>
                      <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">librarian_memory</span></div></li>
                    </ul>
                  </div>
                </li>
              </ul>
            </div>
          </li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>

Folder roles:

- lectures &rarr; lecture slides and course materials
- assignments &rarr; homework and problem sheets
- announcements &rarr; administrative notices
- outputs &rarr; completed work
- librarian_memory &rarr; local AI context

::: {.section-conclusion}
Each course becomes a self-contained workspace.
:::

## Project Workspace
::: {.section-lead}
Projects are stored under the `Projects` directory.
:::

Example:

<div class="file-tree" aria-label="Project workspace examples">
  <ul class="file-tree__root">
    <li class="file-tree__item">
      <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
        <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">Projects</span></button>
        <ul class="file-tree__children">
          <li class="file-tree__item">
            <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
              <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">Personal_Website</span></button>
              <ul class="file-tree__children">
                <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">librarian_memory</span></div></li>
              </ul>
            </div>
          </li>
          <li class="file-tree__item">
            <div class="file-tree__folder is-open">
              <button class="file-tree__row file-tree__toggle" type="button" aria-expanded="true"><span class="file-tree__chevron" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">AI_Research_OS</span></button>
              <ul class="file-tree__children">
                <li class="file-tree__item file-tree__item--folder"><div class="file-tree__row"><span class="file-tree__chevron-spacer" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__icon file-tree__icon--folder" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="file-tree__label">librarian_memory</span></div></li>
              </ul>
            </div>
          </li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </li>
  </ul>
</div>

Project structures are intentionally flexible.

::: {.section-conclusion}
Every project should still include `librarian_memory/` so local AI context survives across sessions and tools.
:::

## Example Workflows
::: {.section-lead}
The architecture becomes clearer when applied to real scenarios.
:::

### Scenario 1 - Automatic Course Material Organization
Course files downloaded from LearnUS can be passed directly to Gemini CLI.

Gemini automatically:

- reads the file
- detects course information
- renames the document
- places it in the correct folder
- updates the course context

Example transformation:

```{.text .file-tree-block}
00. Course Introduction.pdf
&rarr; CAS2101_L00_Course_Introduction.pdf
```

Stored in:

```{.text .file-tree-block}
University/
2026_Spring/
CAS2101_Discrete_Mathematics/
lectures/
```

At the same time Gemini updates:

`librarian_memory/current_task.md`

### Scenario 2 - Project Documentation Management
Software projects rely heavily on GitHub for code.

Code lives in GitHub.

But projects often generate additional materials:

- documentation
- design notes
- architecture diagrams
- manuals

These files are stored in:

```{.text .file-tree-block}
Projects/
project_name/
```

Gemini helps organize these documents while GitHub manages code.

### Scenario 3 - Research Workflow
Academic research follows a structured pipeline.

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Perplexity</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Zotero</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">NotebookLM</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Claude</span>
:::

Steps:

1. Discover papers using Perplexity
2. Store original PDFs in Zotero
3. Analyze documents in NotebookLM
4. Reason about ideas with Claude

Important distinction:

`Zotero = authoritative paper archive`

The user manages Zotero directly.

### Scenario 4 - Shared Context Across AI Tools
AI tools usually lose context between sessions.

This system solves that by storing context in Drive.

Example:

```{.text .file-tree-block}
CAS2101_Discrete_Mathematics/
librarian_memory/
```

Files such as:

- `current_task.md`
- `handoff.md`
- `brief.md`

can be read by multiple AI tools.

Examples:

- Claude
- ChatGPT
- Perplexity

When connected to the Drive workspace, these tools can restore context by reading these files.

This creates persistent cross-AI memory.

### Scenario 5 - Automatic Schedule Extraction
Course documents often contain important dates.

Examples:

- assignment deadlines
- exam dates
- presentations

Gemini CLI can:

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Read document</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Extract dates</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Update Google Calendar</span>
:::

Example:

- Midterm: April 21
- Final: June 16

Gemini can automatically create calendar events such as:

- CAS2101 Midterm
- CAS2101 Final Exam

At the same time, it updates local context.

This connects:

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Documents</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Memory</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Calendar</span>
:::

## Environment Setup
::: {.section-lead}
The system relies on several tools working together.
:::

Detailed installation instructions are intentionally omitted.

If needed, refer to official documentation or ask an AI assistant during setup.

### Step 1 - Identity Layer
Create accounts for the services used in the system.

Required services:

- Google
- GitHub
- OpenAI
- Anthropic
- Perplexity

Strongly Recommended

Use a single Google account as the primary identity.

Many services support Google OAuth login, simplifying authentication.

Benefits:

- fewer credentials
- faster setup
- easier cross-service integration

### Step 2 - Development Environment
Install basic development tools.

- Git
- VS Code
- Node.js or Python
- Terminal environment

### Step 3 - Gemini CLI and Google Drive Integration
Core components:

- Google Cloud project
- gcloud CLI
- gws CLI
- Gemini CLI
- Google Drive for desktop

Recommended setup order:

1. Install Google Drive for desktop
2. Install gcloud CLI
3. Create a Google Cloud project
4. Install and authenticate gws CLI
5. Install Gemini CLI

Result:

::: {.workflow-pipeline}
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Gemini CLI</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">gws</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__arrow" aria-hidden="true">&rarr;</span>
<span class="workflow-pipeline__step">Google Drive</span>
:::

This enables AI-managed shared memory.

### Step 4 - AI Tools
Tools can be accessed through different interfaces.

Web tools

- Perplexity
- NotebookLM

CLI tools

- Gemini CLI
- Codex CLI
- Claude Code

App / Web tools

- ChatGPT
- Claude

Usage depends on workflow preference.

### Step 5 - Optional Infrastructure
Optional but useful tools:

- Zotero
- Docker
- GoodNotes

Typical uses:

- Zotero &rarr; paper archive
- Docker &rarr; reproducible environments
- GoodNotes &rarr; handwritten notes

## Setup Philosophy
::: {.section-lead}
The system avoids tightly coupling all tools.
:::

Instead it relies on loosely connected components through shared resources:

- Google Drive
- GitHub
- local files

::: {.section-conclusion}
This makes the architecture flexible and easy to modify.
:::

## Conclusion
::: {.section-lead}
AI tools are powerful, but using them independently often leads to fragmented workflows and lost context.
:::

This architecture treats AI tools as specialized components within a structured system.

By assigning clear roles to each tool and using Google Drive as a shared memory layer, the system enables:

- organized knowledge
- persistent AI context
- coordinated workflows across tools

::: {.section-conclusion}
The result is a practical environment for learning, research, and software development.
:::

## Repository
A reference implementation of this architecture is available on GitHub.

::: {.repository-card}
[ai-utilization-system](https://github.com/smkgenesis/ai-utilization-system)

The repository includes environment setup guidelines, example folder structures, and documentation of the AI utilization architecture.
:::

::: {.section-conclusion}
Turn a collection of AI tools into a structured working system.
:::
